records.ety: [L. vibratus, p. p. of vibrare, v. t. & v. i., to shake, brandish, vibrate; akin to Skr. vip to tremble, Icel. veifa to wave, vibrate. See Waive and cf. Whip, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vibrated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Vibrating.]

defs: To brandish; to move to and fro; to swing; as, to vibrate a sword or a staff.
To mark or measure by moving to and fro; as, a pendulum vibrating seconds.
To affect with vibratory motion; to set in vibration.
quotes: Breath vocalized, that is, vibrated or undulated, may . . . impress a swift, tremulous motion.

defs: To move to and fro, or from side to side, as a pendulum, an elastic rod, or a stretched string, when disturbed from its position of rest; to swing; to oscillate.
To have the constituent particles move to and fro, with alternate compression and dilation of parts, as the air, or any elastic body; to quiver.
To produce an oscillating or quivering effect of sound; as, a whisper vibrates on the ear.
To pass from one state to another; to waver; to fluctuate; as, a man vibrates between two opinions.
